description: Concept graph for Python programming fundamentals through basic data structures, algorithms, and introductory time complexity. Edit this file to add, remove, or re-link concepts; the graph builder picks up changes on next load.

concepts.name: Running Python
concepts.description: How to execute Python code: the interpreter, scripts (.py files), and the REPL.

concepts.name: Variables and Assignment
concepts.description: Binding names to values with =, naming rules, and reassignment.

concepts.name: Basic Data Types
concepts.description: Built-in primitive types: int, float, str, bool, and None.

concepts.name: Arithmetic and Comparison Operators
concepts.description: Arithmetic (+ - * / // % **) and comparison (== != < > <= >=) operators.

concepts.name: Type Conversion
concepts.description: Converting between types with int(), float(), str(), bool().

concepts.name: Input and Output
concepts.description: Reading from the user with input() and writing with print(); input() always returns a string.

concepts.name: Boolean Logic
concepts.description: Boolean values True/False and the logical operators and, or, not.

concepts.name: If / Elif / Else Statements
concepts.description: Branching execution based on boolean conditions.

concepts.name: While Loops
concepts.description: Repeating a block while a condition remains True; the risk of infinite loops.

concepts.name: For Loops
concepts.description: Iterating over the elements of any iterable (list, string, range, etc.).

concepts.name: The range() Function
concepts.description: Generating arithmetic sequences for indexed iteration: range(stop), range(start, stop), range(start, stop, step).

concepts.name: break and continue
concepts.description: Early loop termination with break and skipping to the next iteration with continue.

concepts.name: Defining Functions
concepts.description: Defining reusable blocks of code with def and calling them.

concepts.name: Parameters and Arguments
concepts.description: The distinction between parameters (in the definition) and arguments (in the call); positional vs keyword arguments.

concepts.name: Return Values
concepts.description: Returning data from a function with return; functions without return implicitly return None.

concepts.name: Default Argument Values
concepts.description: Giving parameters default values so callers may omit them.

concepts.name: Variable Scope
concepts.description: Local vs enclosing vs global vs built-in scopes (LEGB rule).

concepts.name: Lambda Functions
concepts.description: Anonymous one-expression functions defined with lambda.

concepts.name: String Basics
concepts.description: String literals with quotes, escape sequences, and concatenation with +.

concepts.name: String Indexing and Slicing
concepts.description: Accessing characters by index and substrings via slicing; negative indices.

concepts.name: Common String Methods
concepts.description: Methods such as lower(), upper(), strip(), split(), join(), replace(), find().

concepts.name: String Formatting
concepts.description: Building formatted strings with f-strings and str.format().

concepts.name: List Basics
concepts.description: Creating lists with [...], list elements, length, and basic operations.

concepts.name: List Indexing and Slicing
concepts.description: Accessing list elements by index and producing sublists via slicing.

concepts.name: List Methods
concepts.description: Mutating methods such as append(), insert(), remove(), pop(), sort(), reverse().

concepts.name: Iterating Over Lists
concepts.description: Walking a list element by element with a for loop.

concepts.name: List Comprehensions
concepts.description: Concise list construction with [expr for x in iterable if condition].

concepts.name: Tuples
concepts.description: Ordered, fixed-size sequences created with parentheses; commonly used for grouped return values.

concepts.name: Sets
concepts.description: Unordered collections of unique elements created with {...} or set().

concepts.name: Set Operations
concepts.description: Mathematical operations on sets: union, intersection, difference, symmetric difference.

concepts.name: Dictionary Basics
concepts.description: Key/value mappings created with {key: value, ...}; accessing values by key.

concepts.name: Dictionary Methods
concepts.description: Methods such as keys(), values(), items(), get(), update(), pop().

concepts.name: Iterating Over Dictionaries
concepts.description: Looping over keys, values, or items() pairs in a dictionary.

concepts.name: Nested Dictionaries
concepts.description: Dictionaries whose values are themselves dictionaries; navigating multi-level data.

concepts.name: Recursion Basics
concepts.description: Solving a problem by having a function call itself on a smaller instance.

concepts.name: Recursion Base Case
concepts.description: The terminating condition that stops the recursion and returns a direct answer.

concepts.name: Recursive Case
concepts.description: The branch of the function that reduces the problem and recurses; combining sub-results.

concepts.name: Recursion vs Iteration
concepts.description: When to prefer recursion over loops; tradeoffs in clarity and call-stack cost.

concepts.name: Linear Search
concepts.description: Scanning a sequence element by element until a target is found or the end is reached.

concepts.name: Binary Search
concepts.description: Repeatedly halving a sorted sequence to locate a target; faster than linear search.

concepts.name: Bubble Sort
concepts.description: Sorting by repeatedly swapping adjacent out-of-order elements until the list is sorted.

concepts.name: Selection Sort
concepts.description: Sorting by repeatedly selecting the smallest remaining element and placing it at the front.

concepts.name: Introduction to Big O Notation
concepts.description: Describing how an algorithm's running time grows as input size grows, ignoring constants.

concepts.name: Time Complexity Analysis
concepts.description: Counting the dominant operations in code and expressing the result in Big O.

concepts.name: Common Complexity Classes
concepts.description: Understanding O(1), O(log n), O(n), O(n log n), and O(n^2) and where each appears in practice.

concepts.name: Exceptions
concepts.description: What an exception is and the common built-in exception types (ValueError, TypeError, KeyError, etc.).

concepts.name: try / except Blocks
concepts.description: Catching exceptions so the program can recover instead of crashing.

concepts.name: Raising Exceptions
concepts.description: Signalling error conditions yourself with the raise statement.

concepts.name: Importing Modules
concepts.description: Using code from other files or packages with import and from ... import ....

concepts.name: Python Standard Library
concepts.description: Using batteries-included modules such as math, random, os, json without installing anything.
